ucs.title: [LLM] Storm-1811 / Black Basta — Quick Assist (quickassist.exe) spawning LOLBins or scripting hosts
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Storm-1811 hand-on-keyboard step where an attacker, after social-engineering a victim into starting a Microsoft Quick Assist remote-help session, types living-off-the-land commands that spawn cmd / PowerShell / curl / certutil / OpenSSH from quickassist.exe. Microsoft, Rapid7 and Red Canary all observed this exact parent→child shape preceding Black Basta deployment.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as child_cmd values(Processes.process_path) as child_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="quickassist.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","msiexec.exe","ssh.exe","scp.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","netsh.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search NOT user="*$" AND NOT user IN ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort 0 - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let StormChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","msiexec.exe","ssh.exe","scp.exe","schtasks.exe","reg.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","netsh.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "quickassist.exe"
| where FileName in~ (StormChildren)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend SuspiciousIntent = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","DownloadFile","IEX","Invoke-Expression","-EncodedCommand","-enc "), "PS-download/exec",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-urlcache","--insecure"," -k "), "Remote payload fetch",
    FileName =~ "ssh.exe" or FileName =~ "scp.exe", "OpenSSH tunneling (Storm-1811 staple)",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("AntiSpam.bat","updater.bat","clean.bat"), "Storm-1811 batch artefact",
    "Generic LOLBin under Quick Assist"
)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountUpn,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          SuspiciousIntent,
          IsRemoteSession = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let StormChildren = dynamic(["\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\curl.exe","\\certutil.exe","\\mshta.exe","\\rundll32.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\wscript.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\msiexec.exe","\\ssh.exe","\\scp.exe","\\schtasks.exe","\\reg.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe","\\netsh.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\quickassist.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any (StormChildren)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountType == "User"
| extend SuspiciousIntent = case(
    CommandLine has_any ("DownloadString","DownloadFile","IEX","Invoke-Expression","-EncodedCommand","-enc "), "PS-download/exec",
    CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-urlcache","--insecure"," -k "), "Remote payload fetch",
    NewProcessName endswith "\\ssh.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\scp.exe", "OpenSSH tunneling (Storm-1811 staple)",
    CommandLine has_any ("AntiSpam.bat","updater.bat","clean.bat"), "Storm-1811 batch artefact",
    "Generic LOLBin under Quick Assist"
)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, AccountDomain,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, SuspiciousIntent
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Quick Assist Spawning LOLBin or Scripting Host (Storm-1811 / Black Basta)
id: 8e2b3a55-1b4c-4d77-9f5e-storm1811quickassist
status: experimental
description: Detects Microsoft Quick Assist (quickassist.exe) spawning command shells, scripting hosts, download utilities or OpenSSH binaries — the hand-on-keyboard pattern Microsoft attributed to Storm-1811 prior to Black Basta deployment, following email-bomb + fake-helpdesk social engineering.
references:
    - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2024/05/15/threat-actors-misusing-quick-assist-in-social-engineering-attacks-leading-to-ransomware/
    - https://redcanary.com/blog/threat-intelligence/black-basta-quick-assist/
    - https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/2024/05/10/ongoing-social-engineering-campaign-refreshes-payloads/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
    - attack.execution
    - attack.command_and_control
    - attack.t1219.002
    - attack.t1566.004
    - attack.t1059.001
    - attack.t1059.003
    - attack.t1105
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_parent:
        ParentImage|endswith: '\quickassist.exe'
    selection_child:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\pwsh.exe'
            - '\curl.exe'
            - '\certutil.exe'
            - '\mshta.exe'
            - '\rundll32.exe'
            - '\bitsadmin.exe'
            - '\wscript.exe'
            - '\cscript.exe'
            - '\msiexec.exe'
            - '\ssh.exe'
            - '\scp.exe'
            - '\schtasks.exe'
            - '\net.exe'
            - '\net1.exe'
            - '\netsh.exe'
            - '\reg.exe'
    condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
    - Genuine Microsoft helpdesk staff using command-line tools during a remote session (rare, should be allowlisted by approved support account)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Quick Assist parent → shell/LOLBin child is essentially never produced by legitimate Microsoft helpdesk usage — Quick Assist normally only spawns its own UI helpers. Microsoft's May 2024 Storm-1811 advisory and Red Canary's reporting both call out this exact tree as the kinetic step in the Black Basta kill chain (email bomb → vishing as Helpdesk via Teams/phone → user runs Quick Assist → attacker

_field_issues.message: `OpenSSH` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Storm` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`AntiSpam` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Generic` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`LOLBin` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Quick` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Assist` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `OpenSSH` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`Storm` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

`Quick` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
`Assist` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Storm-1811 email-bomb followed by remote-support tool launch on the bombed user's device
ucs.description: Correlates a high-volume inbound spam burst against a single mailbox (Storm-1811's 'email bombing' lure) with the subsequent execution of a remote-support / RMM binary (Quick Assist, MSRA, AnyDesk, ScreenConnect, TeamViewer) on a device owned by that same user within 4 hours — the full Storm-1811 → Black Basta initial-access chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count as InboundCount dc(All_Email.src_user) as UniqueSenders min(_time) as BombStart max(_time) as BombEnd from datamodel=Email.All_Email where All_Email.direction="inbound" by All_Email.recipient _time span=15m | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | where InboundCount>=30 AND UniqueSenders>=20 | eval window_end=BombEnd+14400 | rename recipient as user | join type=inner user [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as ToolEvents values(Processes.process) as ToolCmd values(Processes.process_name) as ToolName min(_time) as ToolTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("quickassist.exe","msra.exe","AnyDesk.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Service.exe","Supremo.exe","NetSupportClient32.exe","client32.exe") by host Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename user as user_short ] | where ToolTime>=BombStart AND ToolTime<=window_end | table BombStart BombEnd ToolTime user host InboundCount UniqueSenders ToolName ToolCmd | sort 0 - ToolTime
ucs.kql:
let WindowMinutes = 15m;
let BombThreshold = 30;
let UniqueSenderThreshold = 20;
let PostBombSeconds = 4h;
let RmmTools = dynamic(["quickassist.exe","msra.exe","anydesk.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","supremo.exe","netsupportclient32.exe","client32.exe"]);
let EmailBomb = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
    | summarize InboundCount = count(),
                UniqueSenders = dcount(SenderFromAddress),
                UniqueSenderDomains = dcount(SenderFromDomain),
                BombStart = min(Timestamp),
                BombEnd = max(Timestamp)
                by RecipientEmailAddress, bin(Timestamp, WindowMinutes)
    | where InboundCount >= BombThreshold and UniqueSenders >= UniqueSenderThreshold;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (RmmTools)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("aka.ms/quickassist","quick assist","join.me","anydesk","screenconnect")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner EmailBomb on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where Timestamp between (BombStart .. BombEnd + PostBombSeconds)
| extend MinutesAfterBomb = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, BombEnd)
| project BombStart, BombEnd, ToolLaunchTime = Timestamp,
          MinutesAfterBomb, DeviceName, AccountUpn,
          BombMessageCount = InboundCount,
          UniqueSenders, UniqueSenderDomains,
          ToolName = FileName, ToolCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ToolParent = InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by ToolLaunchTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let WindowMinutes = 15m;
let BombThreshold = 30;
let UniqueSenderThreshold = 20;
let PostBombSeconds = 4h;
let RmmPaths = dynamic(["\\quickassist.exe","\\msra.exe","\\anydesk.exe","\\screenconnect.clientservice.exe","\\teamviewer.exe","\\teamviewer_service.exe","\\supremo.exe","\\netsupportclient32.exe","\\client32.exe"]);
let EmailBomb = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("Receive","MailItemsAccessed","MessageReceived")
    | where isnotempty(MailboxOwnerUPN)
    | summarize InboundCount = count(),
                UniqueSenders = dcount(Sender),
                BombStart = min(TimeGenerated),
                BombEnd = max(TimeGenerated)
                by MailboxOwnerUPN, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowMinutes)
    | where InboundCount >= BombThreshold and UniqueSenders >= UniqueSenderThreshold;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName has_any (RmmPaths)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountType == "User"
| extend UPN = strcat(AccountName, "@", split(AccountDomain, ".")[0])
| join kind=inner (
    EmailBomb
    | extend UserPrefix = tostring(split(MailboxOwnerUPN, "@")[0])
  ) on $left.AccountName == $right.UserPrefix
| where TimeGenerated between (BombStart .. BombEnd + PostBombSeconds)
| extend MinutesAfterBomb = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, BombEnd)
| project BombStart, BombEnd, ToolLaunchTime = TimeGenerated,
          MinutesAfterBomb, Computer, MailboxOwnerUPN,
          BombMessageCount = InboundCount, UniqueSenders,
          ToolPath = NewProcessName, ToolCmd = CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName
| order by ToolLaunchTime desc
ucs.rationale: The 'email bomb to overwhelm the inbox, then call the user as Helpdesk to run Quick Assist' chain is the singular Storm-1811 fingerprint per the Microsoft (May 2024), Red Canary, and Rapid7 reports — no other tracked actor uses this combo as their primary initial-access pattern. Each leg alone (a noisy mailbox, an RMM launch) is benign; the 4-hour temporal join between the bombed mailbox owner and
